Yui
結
Est. 2019 · Bengaluru

Our Philosophy

Where Japanese
craft meets
Bengaluru.

Yui — meaning "to tie" or "to connect" — was born from a simple conviction: that the most profound flavors arise when tradition and place are allowed to meet.

Every dish travels through the lens of Japanese culinary philosophy — patience, precision, minimalism — before it arrives at your table. We source locally, respect seasonality, and cook with quiet intention.

5+
Years in Bengaluru

01 — Head Chef

Takashi
Mori

Umami & Tradition

Forty years in Osaka's finest kitchens shaped Takashi's understanding of what Japanese cuisine truly means — patience, restraint, and the courage to let a single ingredient speak. He joined Yui at its founding, bringing a philosophy that flavour should never shout.

His ramen broth simmers for 22 hours. His miso takes three months. Every dish carries the quiet confidence of a man who has nothing left to prove.

匠

02 — Pastry & Seasonal

Hana
Yoshida

Lightness & Season

Hana trained at Le Cordon Bleu Tokyo before finding her way back to the ingredients she grew up with — yuzu, matcha, sakura. She leads Yui's seasonal menu, designing dishes that change with each monsoon, harvest, and bloom.

She believes the best Japanese cooking is about listening to the calendar. Her menus are love letters to the present moment, never repeated twice.

華

03 — Omakase & Sushi

Kenji
Nakamura

Precision & Craft

Kenji's hands move with the calm of someone who has made the same cut ten thousand times and still finds something new in it. He studied under a Tsukiji master for eight years before his first solo service.

At Yui he runs the omakase counter three nights a week. No menus. No repeats. Just trust, and the most honest fish Bengaluru has ever tasted.

技
Yui

Modern Japanese cuisine rooted in the art of umami — crafted with patience, served with care.
